summary: First test iteration for DressCircle. 27/27 backend pytest assertions passed against public BASE_URL covering health, auth (login/signup/OTP/me), cities, listings (CRUD, filters, geo distance, trending, view increment), wishlist toggle, full booking lifecycle (create→approve→date-locking→409 conflict→403 unauthorized), phone hidden-before-approval / exposed-after, messages, notifications, and image upload+serve via Emergent Object Storage. Frontend smoke + integration tests via Playwright against live preview URL: landing page renders hero & value props, /auth has all expected data-testids (tab-login/signup/otp, login-email/password/submit, demo-login), email/password login navigates to /feed, JWT persisted in localStorage, Feed shows 12 dress cards with search-input, Filters modal exposes filter-category/size/occasion (filter-price & filter-distance testids missing — minor), dress detail page shows gallery + calendar + duration-3/6/9 + wishlist-btn + request-rent-btn, Request to Rent successfully creates booking and shows success toast 'Request sent! Owner will respond shortly.', wishlist/notifications/dashboard pages reachable, no JS console errors observed.

issues: data-testid='filter-price' and 'filter-distance' missing on price/distance filter controls inside the Filters modal — only filter-category, filter-size, filter-occasion are present. Add testids for completeness.

The 'use demo account' link (data-testid='demo-login') appears to autofill credentials but did not reliably auto-submit on first run — second run via explicit form submit worked. Consider making demo-login click both fill and submit so users land on /feed in one click.

action_items: (Optional) Add data-testid='filter-price' and data-testid='filter-distance' to the price slider and distance/radius slider inside the Feed Filters sheet for better automated testability.
(Optional) Ensure data-testid='demo-login' link both fills credentials AND submits the login (currently navigation didn't always occur after a single click).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is single-file ~734 lines — borderline acceptable for MVP but consider splitting auth/listings/bookings/messages routers into separate modules if more endpoints are added.

/api/wishlist returns listings without indicating which were soft-deleted — currently fine since delete sets status=inactive, but inactive ones are still returned via $in lookup. Consider filtering status==active in get_wishlist.
auth/me hidden phone in get_listing for owner is good. However GET /api/listings/{id} exposes owner.id — fine. Just confirm reviews collection doesn't leak emails/phones.
OTP verify auto-creates a user with empty password_hash — login via password later would silently fail (verify_password handles empty hash gracefully). Acceptable for demo.

init_storage() is called synchronously inside async startup; uses blocking 'requests' instead of httpx — fine for one-shot init but file upload/serve also use blocking requests inside FastAPI handlers. Under load, this can block the event loop. Consider httpx.AsyncClient.
create_booking computes rent_total = rent_price * (duration_days // 3); for duration_days=6 this gives 2x and for 9 it gives 3x — matches PRD (price-per-3-days). Good.
All endpoints exclude _id correctly via projection. JWT 30-day expiry is reasonable for demo.
Frontend file uploads and dress images are served via /api/files/{id}; ensure the storage URL path returned is namespaced under APP_NAME='dresscircle' which it is.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on critical flows (landing, auth, feed, detail, request-rent, wishlist, notifications, dashboard)

test_credentials: demo@dresscircle.in / demo1234 (renter); aanya@dresscircle.in / password123 (owner of seeded listings)
seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded data: 7 users (6 + demo) + 24 listings. Tests create TEST_-prefixed signup users, OTP users, and bookings — these accumulate but don't disrupt seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_dresscircle_api.py is reusable as regression suite. Run via: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_dresscircle_api.py -v. The booking_ctx fixture creates a booking from demo→aanya's first listing on date today+30 — re-runs lock more dates but tests adjust offsets. If many runs accumulate booked dates, reseed via 'python /app/backend/seed.py'. Frontend Playwright flow: explicit login (login-email + login-password + login-submit) is reliable; demo-login auto-fill link is finicky.
rca of the issue: No bugs found; everything working as specified. Only enhancement-level feedback on missing testids and demo-login UX.
